original: The dissatisfied subjects overthrew the monarch and replaced him with a libertarian ruler who respected their democratic principles.
revised: The rebels replaced the monarch with a libertarian ruler who respected democratic principles.

original: The linchpin in the prosecution’s case was the hair from the defendant’s head, which was found at the scene of the crime.
revised: The defendant's hair, found at the scene, was the linchpin of the prosecution's case.

original: Although the dancers were all outstanding, Jae Sun’s control of her lithe body was particularly impressive.
revised: Jae Sun's control of her lithe body impressed every dancer on stage.

original: Because Guenevere’s essay was so lucid, I only had to read it once to understand her reasoning.
revised: Because Guenevere's essay was so lucid, I understood her reasoning after one reading.

original: Gideon’s story, in which he described a character torturing his sister’s dolls, was judged too lurid to be printed in the school’s literary magazine.
revised: Editors rejected Gideon's lurid story of torturing his sister's dolls.

original: Little did the explorers know that as they turned the next bend of the calm river a vicious maelstrom would catch their boat.
revised: Around the river bend, a vicious maelstrom seized the explorers' boat.

original: Although I had already broken most of her dishes, Jacqueline was magnanimous enough to continue letting me use them.
revised: Jacqueline was magnanimous enough to keep lending me dishes I had broken.

original: When I was arrested for speeding, I screamed maledictions against the policeman and the entire police department.
revised: After my arrest, I shouted a malediction at the officer and his department.

original: Maximillian’s political opinions were so malleable that anyone he talked to was able to change his mind instantly.
revised: Maximillian's opinions were so malleable that any conversation changed his mind instantly.

original: When I wrote the wrong sum on the chalkboard, my mistake was so manifest that the entire class burst into laughter.
revised: My mistake was so manifest that the entire class burst out laughing.

original: The popularity of Dante’s Inferno is partly due to the fact that the work allows for manifold interpretations.
revised: The poem's popularity stems partly from its allowance for manifold interpretations.

original: Although some nineteenthcentury critics viewed Dickens’s writing as mawkish, contemporary readers have found great emotional depth in his works.
revised: Some nineteenth-century critics found Dickens's writing mawkish, but modern readers see depth.

original: Though he was widely respected for his mathematical proofs, the mercurial genius was impossible to live with.
revised: Respected for his proofs, the mercurial genius remained impossible to live with.

original: Winnifred went to the gym every day for a year and underwent a metamorphosis from a waiflike girl to an athletic woman.
revised: After a year at the gym, Winnifred underwent a metamorphosis from waif to athlete.

original: Refusing to display even a modicum of sensitivity, Henrietta announced her boss’s affair in front of the entire office.
revised: Refusing even a modicum of sensitivity, Henrietta announced her boss's affair publicly.

original: Mores change over time; many things that were tolerated in 1975 are no longer seen as being socially acceptable.
revised: Mores change over time; many things tolerated in 1975 seem unacceptable today.

original: This Swiss Army knife has multifarious functions and capabilities. Among other things, it can act as a knife, a saw, a toothpick, and a slingshot.
revised: This Swiss Army knife has multifarious tools, including a knife, saw, and sling.

original: He is more concerned with the mundane issues of day-to-day life than with spiritual topics.
revised: He worries more about mundane daily chores than about spiritual matters.

original: It was difficult to decide what to do Friday night because the city presented us with myriad possibilities for fun.
revised: On Friday night the city offered us myriad possibilities for entertainment.

original: My day was boring, but the nadir came when I accidentally spilled a bowl of spaghetti on my head.
revised: My dull day reached its nadir when spaghetti spilled onto my head.

original: Unfortunately, my brilliant paper was only in its nascent form on the morning that it was due.
revised: Unfortunately, my brilliant paper existed only in nascent form on the due date.

original: Although Dr. Meanman’s nefarious plot to melt the polar icecaps was terrifying, it was so impractical that nobody really worried about it.
revised: Dr. Meanman's nefarious plan to melt the ice caps was too impractical to fear.

original: Jessie’s grandfather called me a negligent fool after I left the door to his apartment unlocked even though there had been a recent string of robberies.
revised: After I left his door unlocked amid recent robberies, Jessie's grandfather called me negligent.

original: As a neophyte in the literary world, Malik had trouble finding a publisher for his first novel.
revised: As a neophyte novelist, Malik struggled to find a publisher for his work.

original: In the first six months after college, Jose led a nomadic life, living in New York, California, and Idaho.
revised: After college Jose lived a nomadic life in New York, California, and Idaho.

original: Because he was moving the following week and needed to get rid of his furniture more than he needed money, Jordan sold everything for a nominal fee.
revised: Moving soon, Jordan sold his furniture for a nominal fee rather than profit.

original: Although deep down she was very angry, Marsha acted in a nonchalant manner when she found out that her best friend had used her clothing without asking.
revised: Though angry inside, Marsha acted nonchalant when her friend borrowed her clothes.

original: The nuances of the poem were not obvious to the casual reader, but the professor was able to point them out.
revised: The poem's nuance escaped casual readers but not the professor's careful explanation.

original: Although Serena had never watered the plant, which was about to die, Javier was able to nurture it back to life.
revised: Though Serena never watered the dying plant, Javier managed to nurture it back.

original: Oblivious to the burning smell emanating from the kitchen, my father did not notice that the rolls in the oven were burned until much too late.
revised: Oblivious to the kitchen smoke, my father failed to notice the burned rolls.

original: With the inventions of tape decks and CDs, which both have better sound and are easier to use, eight-track players are now entirely obsolete.
revised: With better tapes and CDs available, eight-track players have become entirely obsolete.

original: Political opponents warned that the prime minister’s obtuse approach to foreign policy would embroil the nation in mindless war.
revised: Critics warned that the prime minister's obtuse foreign policy would invite needless war.

original: The fortuneteller’s ominous words flashed through my mind as the hooded figure approached me in the alley.
revised: The fortuneteller's ominous warning returned as a hooded figure approached in the alley.

original: My parents lamented that the pleasures of living in a beautiful country estate no longer outweighed the onerous mortgage payments.
revised: My parents decided the estate's beauty no longer outweighed its onerous mortgage payments.

original: The opulent furnishings of the dictator’s private compound contrasted harshly with the meager accommodations of her subjects.
revised: The dictator's opulent compound contrasted sharply with her subjects' meager housing.

original: The prime minister was visibly shaken when the unruly parliament interrupted his oration about failed domestic policies.
revised: The prime minister faltered when parliament interrupted his oration on failed domestic policies.

original: The ornate styling of the new model of luxury car could not compensate for the poor quality of its motor.
revised: The luxury car's ornate styling could not hide the poor quality of its engine.

original: The company’s profits dwindled because the management pursued orthodox business policies that were incompatible with new industrial trends.
revised: Profits fell because management clung to orthodox policies ill suited to new trends.

original: My uncle oscillated between buying a station wagon to transport his family and buying a sports car to satisfy his boyhood fantasies.
revised: My uncle oscillated between buying a family station wagon and a sports car.

original: Jack’s ostensible reason for driving was that airfare was too expensive, but in reality, he was afraid of flying.
revised: Jack's ostensible reason was cheap airfare, but in truth he feared flying.

original: On the palace tour, the guide focused on the ostentatious decorations and spoke little of the royal family’s history.
revised: On the palace tour the guide dwelt on ostentatious décor, not royal history.

original: The palette of colors utilized in the painting was equaled only by the range of intense emotions the piece evoked.
revised: The painting's palette of colors matched the intense emotions the work evoked.

original: Dr. Van Helsing feared that Lucy’s pallid complexion was due to an unexplained loss of blood.
revised: Dr. Van Helsing feared Lucy's pallid complexion signaled an unexplained loss of blood.

original: Because the new SUV was so popular, it became the paradigm upon which all others were modeled.
revised: Because the new SUV sold so well, it became the paradigm for rivals.

original: The diplomat refused to acknowledge the paradox that negotiating a peace treaty would demand more resources than waging war.
revised: The diplomat denied the paradox that peace talks could cost more than war.

original: A hush fell over the classroom when the teacher returned to find Deborah acting out a parody of his teaching style.
revised: The class fell silent when Deborah performed a parody of the teacher's mannerisms.

original: The king did not believe that his rival could round up enough partisans to overthrow the monarchy.
revised: The king doubted his rival could rally enough partisans to topple the monarchy.

original: The evening’s headline news covered an international scandal caused by a pejorative statement the famous senator had made in reference to a foreign leader.
revised: Headlines blazed after the senator's pejorative remark about a foreign leader sparked scandal.

original: Wishing his book to be pellucid to the common man, Albert Camus avoided using complicated grammar when composing The Stranger.
revised: Seeking pellucid prose for common readers, Camus avoided convoluted grammar in The Stranger.

original: The jury’s verdict may have been more lenient if the criminal had appeared penitent for his gruesome crimes.
revised: The jury might have been lenient had the criminal seemed penitent about his crimes.

original: Having smoked the penultimate cigarette remaining in the pack, Cybil discarded the last cigarette and resolved to quit smoking.
revised: Having smoked the penultimate cigarette in the pack, Cybil threw away the last.

original: Stella complained that her husband’s penurious ways made it impossible to live the lifestyle she felt she deserved.
revised: Stella complained that her husband's penurious habits blocked the lifestyle she deserved.

original: After the official was caught selling government secrets to enemy agents, he was executed for his perfidious ways.
revised: Caught selling secrets to enemy agents, the official was executed for perfidious treason.

original: The new government feared that the Communist sympathizers would have a pernicious influence on the nation’s stability.
revised: The new government feared Communist sympathizers would exert a pernicious influence on stability.

original: The detective was too humble to acknowledge that his perspicacity was the reason for his professional success.
revised: Too humble to admit it, the detective credited his perspicacity for his success.

original: My parents forgave Sandra’s pert humor at the dinner table because it had been so long since they had last seen her.
revised: My parents forgave Sandra's pert humor at dinner because they had missed her.

original: Harry’s parents were frustrated with his pertinacious insistence that a monster lived in his closet. Then they opened the closet door and were eaten.
revised: Harry's parents tired of his pertinacious claim that a monster lived in his closet.

original: Stepping off the plane in Havana, I recognized the pervasive odor of sugar cane fields on fire.
revised: Stepping off the plane in Havana, I smelled the pervasive odor of burning cane.

original: Many people felt that the billionaire’s decision to donate her fortune to house the homeless was the ultimate philanthropic act.
revised: Donating her fortune to house the homeless was widely hailed as a philanthropic act.

original: Monique feared her dog was ill after the animal’s phlegmatic response to his favorite chew toy.
revised: Monique feared her dog was sick when he showed a phlegmatic response to his toy.

original: Book reviewers declared that the author’s new novel was extraordinary and probably the pinnacle of Western literature.
revised: Reviewers declared the author's new novel the pinnacle of recent Western literature.

original: Josh complained that he was paid a pittance for the great amount of work he did at the firm.
revised: Josh complained that the firm paid him a pittance for so much work.

original: After reading over her paper, Helene concluded that what she thought were profound insights were actually just platitudes.
revised: After rereading her paper, Helene realized her insights were mere platitudes.

original: He studied all the data and then came up with a plausible theory that took all factors into account.
revised: He studied all the data and then proposed a plausible theory that fit every factor.

original: When a black cat crossed my sister’s path while she was walking to school, she took it as a portent that she would do badly on her spelling test.
revised: When a black cat crossed her path, my sister read it as a portent of failure.

original: During sea voyages it is essential that ships carry a supply of potable water because salty ocean water makes anyone who drinks it sick.
revised: On long voyages ships must carry potable water because ocean water makes sailors ill.

original: All the villagers stood along the town’s main road to observe as the potentate’s procession headed towards the capital.
revised: Villagers lined the road to watch the potentate's procession head toward the capital.

original: The politician argued that while increased security measures might not fit with the lofty ideals of the nation, they were a pragmatic necessity to ensure everyone’s safety.
revised: The politician admitted that tighter security was a pragmatic necessity despite the nation's ideals.

original: Derek was so academically precocious that by the time he was 10 years old, he was already in the ninth grade.
revised: So academically precocious was Derek that at ten he already attended ninth grade.

original: Francois has a predilection for eating scrambled eggs with ketchup, though I prefer to eat eggs without any condiments.
revised: Francois has a predilection for scrambled eggs with ketchup, unlike my plain preference.

original: When my uncle’s old war injury ached, he interpreted it as a presage of bad weather approaching.
revised: When my uncle's old war wound ached, he took it as a presage of storms.

original: Questioning the fortune cookie’s prediction, Ray went in search of the old hermit who was rumored to be prescient.
revised: Doubting the fortune cookie, Ray sought the hermit rumored to be prescient.

original: The duke prescribed that from this point further all of the peasants living on his lands would have to pay higher taxes.
revised: The duke prescribed that peasants on his lands would henceforth pay higher taxes.

original: The princess grew angry after the presumptuous noble tried to kiss her, even though he was far below her in social status.
revised: The princess recoiled when a presumptuous noble tried to kiss her despite low rank.

original: Though he actually wanted to use his parents’ car to go on a date, Nick borrowed his parents’ car under the pretense of attending a group study session.
revised: Wanting the car for a date, Nick borrowed it under the pretense of studying.

original: The first primates to walk on two legs, called Australopithecus, were the primeval descendants of modern man.
revised: The first bipedal primates called Australopithecus were primeval ancestors of modern humans.

original: After decades of rule by an oppressive government that saw nothing wrong with stealing from its citizens, the recent drought only increased the people’s privation.
revised: After decades of theft by an oppressive regime, the drought deepened the people's privation.

original: Because he was never viewed as a man of great probity, no one was surprised by Mr. Samson’s immoral behavior.
revised: Because few saw him as a man of probity, no one was shocked by his misconduct.

original: In a sick twist of fate, Harold’s childhood proclivity for torturing small animals grew into a desire to become a surgeon.
revised: In a grim twist, Harold's childhood proclivity for harming animals led toward surgery.

original: Jacob’s profane act of dumping frogs in the holy water in the chapel at his boarding school resulted in his dismissal.
revised: Jacob's profane prank of dropping frogs in chapel holy water got him expelled.
